get.summary: Get Symptom Definitions
get.description: NOTE: When making an XML request, make sure the statKey field does not contain any key name with spaces.

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: The adapter kind identifier of the symptom definitions to search for

schema.description: The adapter kind identifier of the symptom definitions to search for

parameters.name: resourceKind
parameters.description: The resource kind identifier of the symptom definitions to search for

schema.description: The resource kind identifier of the symptom definitions to search for

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier(s) of the symptom definitions to search for.  Exact match.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The substring of the symptom definition's name to search for.  Case-insensitive.

schema.description: The substring of the symptom definition's name to search for.  Case-insensitive.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>95</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>70</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>50</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:numericPropertyCondition">
                <ops:operator>LT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:key>system|installed_memGB</ops:key>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
                <ops:value>8.0</ops:value>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="NONE">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:messageEventCondition" eventType="RESOURCE_DOWN" operator="CONTAINS">
                <ops:message>message event conditions are of eventType NOTIFICATION, RESOURCE_DOWN, SYSTEM_DEGRADATION, and compare message to event message via EQ or CONTAINS operator</ops:message>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="UNKNOWN">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:metricEventCondition">
                <ops:hardThresholdEventType>ABOVE</ops:hardThresholdEventType>
                <ops:statKey>cpu|demandmhz</ops:statKey>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-1" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine">
        <ops:name>CPU Too High - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:faultCondition">
                <ops:faultKey>FaultKey - FaultKey matches to a FaultEvent's FaultState</ops:faultKey>
                <ops:faultEvents>FaultEvent1 FaultEvent2</ops:faultEvents>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>95</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="IMMEDIATE">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>70</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
                <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:value>50</ops:value>
                <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:stringPropertySymptomCondition">
                <ops:operator>EQ</ops:operator>
                <ops:key>system|power_state</ops:key>
                <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
                <ops:stringValue>PoweredOff</ops:stringValue>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:messageEventCondition" eventType="RESOURCE_DOWN" operator="CONTAINS">
                <ops:message>message event conditions are of eventType NOTIFICATION, RESOURCE_DOWN, SYSTEM_DEGRADATION, and compare message to event message via EQ or CONTAINS operator</ops:message>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
    <ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem">
        <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
        <ops:state severity="WARNING">
            <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:metricEventCondition">
                <ops:hardThresholdEventType>ABOVE</ops:hardThresholdEventType>
                <ops:statKey>cpu|demandmhz</ops:statKey>
            </ops:condition>
        </ops:state>
    </ops:symptom-definition>
</ops:symptom-definitions>

put.summary: Update an existing Symptom Definition
put.description: Update an existing Symptom Definition

Symptom Definition with LOG Condition.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-733e43f5-092a-4df9-99d7-e8b871c4cae0" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:name>Log based symptom definition</ops:name>
    <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
        <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:logCondition">
            <ops:logTriggerCondition>
                <ops:functionType>COUNT</ops:functionType>
                <ops:value>2.0</ops:value>
                <ops:operatorType>GREATER_THAN</ops:operatorType>
                <ops:timeInterval>5</ops:timeInterval>
                <ops:groupBy/>
            </ops:logTriggerCondition>
            <ops:queryTexts>
                <ops:queryText>filter_1</ops:queryText>
                <ops:queryText>filter_2</ops:queryText>
            </ops:queryTexts>
        </ops:condition>
    </ops:state>
</ops:symptom-definition>

post.summary: Create the Symptom Definition
post.description:
The symptom definition identifier must be null and will be generated by the system.
A symptom definition contains a symptom state, there may only be one symptom state per symptom definition.

get.summary: Get a collection of SuperMetrics based on search parameters
get.description: Get a collection of SuperMetrics based on search parameters

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: SuperMetrics based on identifiers

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: SuperMetrics based on names

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

200.description: The SuperMetrics for the given query

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:supermetrics xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:supermetric>
        <ops:id>0cbdde06-cc02-4d36-9036-69b978c5d79a</ops:id>
        <ops:name>Example SuperMetric for DataCenter</ops:name>
        <ops:formula>sumN(\$This:cpu|demandmhz,2)</ops:formula>
        <ops:description>Sum the CPU usage in MHZ on the assigned resource and its children</ops:description>
    </ops:supermetric>
    <ops:supermetric>
        <ops:id>04a5286c-5495-4c39-8abb-70712ca3aacb</ops:id>
        <ops:name>Example SuperMetric for DataCenter</ops:name>
        <ops:formula>sumN(\$This:cpu|demandmhz,2)</ops:formula>
        <ops:description>Sum the CPU usage in MHZ on the assigned resource and its children</ops:description>
    </ops:supermetric>
</ops:supermetrics>

put.summary: Update a Resource in the system
put.description:
Implementation Notes:


  • When updating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique and required
    are not specified, the API would return an error with HTTP status code of 500
    and an error message indicating the set of missing Resource Identifiers.

  • When updating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique but not required
    are not specified, the Resource is updated where the uniquely identifying Resource Identifiers
    that were not specified will have their value as an empty string.

requestBody.description: The Resource object that needs to be updated

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available
No Custom Example is Available

500.description: The ResourceKey of the Resource is missing the uniquely identifying and required Resource Identifiers

404.description: No Resource is found in the system corresponding to the identifier in the Request Body

put.summary: Set (Replace) Resource as RelationshipType of a specific Resource
put.description:
This API exposes replace semantics. Therefore, all the existing relationships of the
specified relationshipType will be removed and replaced with the resources
specified as part of the request body.

  • If at least a few of the Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    are valid resources then the operation is performed.

  • If there are few Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    that will result in a cyclical relationship, then the operation is aborted.

NOTE: Setting relationship is not synchronous.
As a result, the set operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the source resource

parameters.name: relationshipType
parameters.description: The type of relationship of the objects; Uses the RelationshipType.representation value

requestBody.description: UUID of resources to set as related to this resource

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:uuid-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:uuids>181a78a5-73aa-4a9f-9efa-b2f0431bf68d b9542c28-33c0-43e6-957e-23397f992b67</ops:uuids>
</ops:uuid-values>

204.description: The relationship is set successfully

404.description: All of the Resource that are part of the relatedIds list are invalid/non-existent

post.summary: Add relationships of given type to the resource with specified resourceId
post.description:
• If at least a few of the Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    are valid resources then the operation is performed.

  • If there are few Resource that are part of relatedIds list
    that will result in a cyclical relationship, then those resources will be skipped.

NOTE: Adding relationship is not synchronous. As a
result, the add operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the source resource

parameters.name: relationshipType
parameters.description: The type of relationship of the objects; Uses the RelationshipType.representation value

requestBody.description: UUID of resources to add as related to this resource

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:uuid-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:uuids>139d60a8-cfc7-4460-9829-c4c09bb258d3 b80e279f-15ac-4cfe-886a-c556f489cafa</ops:uuids>
</ops:uuid-values>

204.description: The relationship is added successfully

404.description: All of the Resource that are part of the relatedIds list are invalid/non-existent

put.summary: Put the specific Resource in Maintenance
put.description:
The Resource can end up in two maintenance states - MAINTAINED OR MAINTAINED_MANUAL -
depending upon the inputs specified.

  • If duration/end time is specified, the resource will be placed in MAINTAINED
    state and after the duration/end time expires, the resource state is automatically set to the state
    it was in before entering the maintenance window.
  • If duration/end time is not specified, the resource will be placed in MAINTAINED_MANUAL state.
    Callers have to execute DELETE /suite-api/api/resources/{id}/maintained API to set the Resource
    back to whatever state it was in.
  • If both duration and end time are specified, end time takes preference over duration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Resource identifier

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Duration for which this resource will be in maintenance (In Minutes)

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End time of the maintenance window as a long value (Epoch time)

200.description: The resource is put in maintenance successfully

delete.summary: Bring the Resource out of Maintenance manually
delete.description: Bring the Resource out of Maintenance manually

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Resource identifier

200.description: The resource is brought out from maintenance successfully

not specified, then all of the Adapters will stop monitoring the specified Resources.

put.summary: Put all the specified Resource in Maintenance
put.description:
The Resources can end up in two maintenance states - MAINTAINED OR MAINTAINED_MANUAL -
depending upon the inputs specified.


  • If duration/end time is specified, the Resources will be placed in MAINTAINED
    state and after the duration/end time expires, the state of the Resources would be automatically
    set to the state they were in before entering the maintenance window.
  • If duration/end time is not specified, the Resources will be placed in MAINTAINED_MANUAL state.
    Callers have to execute DELETE /suite-api/api/resources/maintained?id=ID1&id=ID2 API
    to set the Resources back to whatever state they were in.
  • If both duration and end time are specified, end time takes preference over duration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Resource identifiers

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: The Duration for which this resource will be in maintenance (In Minutes)

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The End time of the maintenance window as a long value (Epoch time)

200.description: The resource is put in maintenance successfully

delete.description: Bring the specified set of Resources out of Maintenance manually

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A collection of Resource identifiers

200.description: The resource is brought out from maintenance successfully

application/xml.example:

        <ops:resourceKey>
            <ops:name>Sample Custom Group with Metric Key Condition</ops:name>
            <ops:adapterKindKey>Container</ops:adapterKindKey>
            <ops:resourceKindKey>Environment</ops:resourceKindKey>
        </ops:resourceKey>
        <ops:membershipDefinition>
            <ops:includedResources></ops:includedResources>
            <ops:excludedResources></ops:excludedResources>
            <ops:custom-group-properties/>
            <ops:membership-rule-group>
                <ops:resourceKindKey>
                    <ops:resourceKind>VirtualMachine</ops:resourceKind>
                    <ops:adapterKind>VMWARE</ops:adapterKind>
                </ops:resourceKindKey>

                    <ops:compareOperator>GT</ops:compareOperator>

</ops:cgroups>

post.description: See different request examples with Metric Key, Property Key, Relationship and Resource Name Condition.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:custom-group autoResolveMembership="true" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:resourceKey>
        <ops:name>Sample Custom Group with Metric Key Condition</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>Container</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>Environment</ops:resourceKindKey>
    </ops:resourceKey>
    <ops:membershipDefinition>
        <ops:includedResources></ops:includedResources>
        <ops:excludedResources></ops:excludedResources>
        <ops:custom-group-properties/>
        <ops:membership-rule-group>
            <ops:resourceKindKey>
                <ops:resourceKind>VirtualMachine</ops:resourceKind>
                <ops:adapterKind>VMWARE</ops:adapterKind>
            </ops:resourceKindKey>
            <ops:statConditionRules key="cpu|usage_average">
                <ops:doubleValue>80.0</ops:doubleValue>
                <ops:compareOperator>GT</ops:compareOperator>
            </ops:statConditionRules>
            <ops:propertyConditionRules key="config|hardware|numCpu">
                <ops:doubleValue>4.0</ops:doubleValue>
                <ops:compareOperator>LT</ops:compareOperator>
            </ops:propertyConditionRules>
            <ops:resourceNameConditionRules name="VSAN" compareOperator="NOT_CONTAINS"/>
            <ops:relationshipConditionRules relation="CHILD" name="SampleStorage" compareOperator="CONTAINS" travesalSpecId="vSphere Storage-VMWARE-vSphere World"/>
            <ops:resourceTagConditionRules category="VMFolder" compareOperator="NOT_CONTAINS" stringValue="TestVMFolder"/>
        </ops:membership-rule-group>
    </ops:membershipDefinition>
</ops:custom-group>
No Custom Example is Available

delete.summary: Delete custom datacenters
delete.description:
Delete custom datacenters.
NOTE: Deletion of custom datacenters is not synchronous. As a result,
the delete operation may not happen immediately. Furthermore, there is no way
to track the status of the deletion operation. It is recommended to query back
the system and ensure that the resources (datacenters) with given ids are not present any more.
NOTE: This API is only for custom datacenters deletion. Any invalid or none custom datacenter ids in the request body will be ignored.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:uuid-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:uuids>8703b50b-0c02-4e1f-b29a-66d80a18c218 0a964265-6ee2-4463-b629-d12ab9e5a5ff</ops:uuids>
</ops:uuid-values>

204.description: Custom datacenter was deleted successfully

parameters.name: pageSize

post.summary: Create a Report Schedule to generate a Report for the specified Report Definition and Resource(s)
post.description:
To specify the time zone add X-Ops-API-Timezone header with value of format e.g. Asia/Yerevan.
All the schedules created using this API will be configured to use GMT time zone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Report Definition identifier

parameters.name: X-Ops-API-Timezone
parameters.description:
Custom HTTP header used by Ops API clients to specify Timezone information.
This Timezone value will be typically used for Report Generation.
Either an abbreviation such as "GMT", "PDT" can be specified,
or a full name such as "America/Los_Angeles". Other
formats and representations are not supported.

is applicable to the resource of the specified adapter kind.

is applicable to the resource of the specified adapter kind.

put.summary: Set up your business intent to place VMs based on criteria
put.description:
To set host level placement constraints, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations will automatically create and manage DRS rules. All conflicting user-created DRS rules need to be DEACTIVATED. These include the following:
  • VM-VM affinity / anti-affinity

  • VM-Host affinity / anti-affinity

parameters.name: dataCenterId
parameters.description: Identifier of the dataCenter

parameters.name: deactivateDRSRules
parameters.description: During HOST_BASED configuration, set this flag to true to deactivate all conflicting user-created DRS rules

                <ops:resourceTag category="tag_category_1" name="tag_name_1"/>
                <ops:resourceTag category="tag_category_1" name="tag_name_2"/>
            </ops:resourceTags>
        </ops:tagGroup>
        <ops:tagGroup>
            <ops:name>Network</ops:name>
            <ops:prioritized>false</ops:prioritized>
            <ops:tags/>
            <ops:resourceTags/>
        </ops:tagGroup>
    </ops:tagGroups>
    <ops:excludedClusterTagGroups>

            <ops:name>Operating System</ops:name>

                <ops:resourceTag category="tag_category_2" name="tag_name_2"/>

put.summary: Enable cluster-level optimization across datacenter boundaries in a vCenter
put.description:
If your custom datacenter includes clusters from multiple datacenters within the same vCenter, you can enable cross datacenter move to ensure optimization occurs across datacenter boundaries.
NOTE: This API is only applicable for custom datacenters

parameters.description: ID of the dataCenter

put.summary: Disable cluster-level optimization across datacenter boundaries in a vCenter
put.description:
Disable cluster-level optimization across datacenter boundaries in a vCenter.
NOTE: This API is only applicable for custom datacenters

parameters.name: dataCenterId
parameters.description: ID of the dataCenter

put.summary: Turning on automation
put.description:
Enabling automation will allow the system to optimize your datacenter by continuously looking for optimization opportunities
NOTE: Any scheduled optimizations will be deactivated during the time

parameters.name: dataCenterId
parameters.description: ID of the dataCenter

put.summary: Turning off automation
put.description:
If you disable automation, the system will still look for optimization opportunities. Any actions will now be manual
NOTE: If you have any scheduled optimizations, they will be re-activated

parameters.name: dataCenterId
parameters.description: ID of the dataCenter

get.summary: Query for all maintenance schedules based on schedule id, schedule name and resource id
get.description: Using this method, you can get all maintenance schedules with or without specific parameters

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Maintenance schedule identifiers

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Maintenance schedule names or keys

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: List of Object identifiers

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

200.description: The Maintenance Schedules that match the criteria specified

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:maintenance-schedules xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="ffb93b7f-66da-49fd-aed3-b83cbbbe93e0" key="daily-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>3</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>0</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>180</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>DAILY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:recurrence>1</ops:recurrence>
            <ops:expirationDate>01/20/2050</ops:expirationDate>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="2287e27e-2394-4e12-a11d-d2f535dc9648" key="monthly-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>3</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>0</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>3</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>MONTHLY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:daysOfTheMonth>1 2</ops:daysOfTheMonth>
            <ops:months>1</ops:months>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
            <ops:expireRuns>3</ops:expireRuns>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
    <ops:maintenance-schedule id="2ba16620-a37f-4098-b393-a3b877a3769a" key="weekly-ms1">
        <ops:schedule>
            <ops:hour>15</ops:hour>
            <ops:minuteOfTheHour>3</ops:minuteOfTheHour>
            <ops:duration>1</ops:duration>
            <ops:scheduleType>WEEKLY</ops:scheduleType>
            <ops:recurrence>5</ops:recurrence>
            <ops:daysOfTheWeek>SATURDAY SUNDAY</ops:daysOfTheWeek>
            <ops:timeZone>Asia/Yerevan</ops:timeZone>
            <ops:expireRuns>7</ops:expireRuns>
        </ops:schedule>
    </ops:maintenance-schedule>
</ops:maintenance-schedules>

post.summary: Create a new local user group or import SSO/VIDM/LDAP/AD/VIDB user groups
post.description:
If the authSourceId is specified(in the request), then the user group will be imported from the corresponding auth source (LDAP/AD/SSO/VIDM/VIDB).Note that the id of the user group has to be null.
For LDAP/AD groups the distinguishedName should be provided in the name field.
The value of displayName is used only while importing LDAP/AD groups, and if it is not provided then the value of name will be assigned to it. For SSO/VIDM groups the value of name is assigned to displayName.
NOTE: Before importing please make sure that the group exists in the specified authSource by using the following API - /api/auth/sources/{id}/usergroups/search . Otherwise, if you try to import a non-existing group, a new one will be created with the specified authSourceId.
NOTE: In the case of vIDB user group, the import occurs based on the provided externalId. If other provided details are incorrect, they will be asynchronously updated with the correct values right after the import operation is completed.

requestBody.description: The group object to be created.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>

delete.summary: Delete a list of user groups using their identifiers

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifiers of the local user group objects

204.description: The specified user groups are deleted successfully

put.summary: (Deprecated) Assign a traversal specification to a particular user
put.description:
Traversal specifications assigned through this API will add the specified traversal specification to a role permission for the ReadOnly role. It will also update the user's existing role permissions with this traversal specification.
This API is replaced by the assignRolePermissionToUser and unassignRolePermissionFromUser APIs which are PUT and DELETE operations respectively at the /auth/usergroups/{groupId}/permissions endpoint.Note: mixing current and deprecated APIs is not recommended and may result in undefined behavior.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Identifier of the user group to which the traversal spec needs to be assigned

requestBody.description: The traversal specification to assign to the user group

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

200.description: The traversal spec is assigned to the user group successfully

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

No Custom Example is Available

get.summary: Get all the available authentication sources in the system
get.description:
Get all the available authentication sources in the system.
NOTE: This API works both for authenticated and un-authenticated access. In case of authenticated access (by providing the Authorization header) it will return more information including auth source properties. Please also note that while triggering the API through Swagger interface it will work in un-authenticated scenario (Swagger will not send the Authorization header).

put.summary: Update an authentication source in the system
put.description: If the host is an ldap host and if host/port is changed, then the response will contain a list of certificates found matching with the host. It is the duty of the caller to call the PATCH (/sources) api with the certificate details. Once the PATCH api call is successful, the authentication source is available for use.

requestBody.description: AuthSource object that contains authSource's ID, type, its properties and list of certificate details if ssl is enabled

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:auth-source created="1744473854706" lastModified="1744473854706" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:id>00000000-0000-000a-0000-000000000014</ops:id>
    <ops:name>TEST</ops:name>
    <ops:sourceType id="ACTIVE_DIRECTORY">
        <ops:name>ACTIVE_DIRECTORY</ops:name>
        <ops:description>DESC</ops:description>
    </ops:sourceType>
    <ops:name-value name="display-name">Test_11</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="host">brlrootdc01.vmware.com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="domain">vmware.com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="host-auto-select">false</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="use-ssl">true</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="user-name">username@vmware.com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="password">password</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="base-domain">dc=vmware,dc=com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="common-name">userPrincipalName</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="port">636</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="group-search-criteria">(|(objectClass=group)(objectClass=groupOfNames))</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="member-attribute">member</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="user-search-criteria"></ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="member-match-field"></ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="ldap-context-attributes"></ops:name-value>
</ops:auth-source>

post.summary: Add a new authentication source in the system
post.description: If the authentication source is of type LDAP or AD and if ssl is enabled, the response object will contain a list of certificates found matching with the provided host.If the authentication source is not of type LDAP or AD (or) if ssl is not enabled, then the authentication source is available for use as soon as the api call is successful.It is the duty of the caller to call the PATCH (/sources) api with the certificate details. Once the PATCH api call is successful, the authentication source is available for use.

requestBody.description: Contains authSource's type and its properties

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:auth-source created="1744473855784" lastModified="1744473855784" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    <ops:name-value name="domain">vmware.com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="host-auto-select">false</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="use-ssl">true</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="user-name">username@vmware.com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="password">password</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="base-domain">dc=vmware,dc=com</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="common-name">userPrincipalName</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="port">636</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="group-search-criteria">(|(objectClass=group)(objectClass=groupOfNames))</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="member-attribute">member</ops:name-value>

    <ops:name-value name="member-match-field"></ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="ldap-context-attributes"></ops:name-value>
</ops:auth-source>

get.summary: Get all scopes
get.description: Get all scopes available in the environment

get.summary: Get the privileges for a user role
get.description: Get the privileges for a user role

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The name of the role

200.description: The list of privileges for the specified user role

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>

    <ops:privilege key="ManageAlerts">
        <ops:name>Manage Alerts</ops:name>
        <ops:description>Manage and view Alerts in the system</ops:description>
    </ops:privilege>
    <ops:privilege key="ManageAlarms">
        <ops:name>Manage Alarms</ops:name>
        <ops:description>Manage and view Alarms in the system</ops:description>
    </ops:privilege>
</ops:privileges>

put.summary: Remove a vCenter from Application Monitoring Configuration
put.description: NOTE: Application Monitoring configuration will be deleted after removing last mapped vCenter from it.

parameters.name: appMonitoringUrl
parameters.description: Url of the application monitoring

parameters.name: vcUrl
parameters.description: The url of the vCenter to be removed

200.description: The vCenter is removed from the configuration successfully

put.summary: Add a vCenter to be monitored by the specified application monitoring configuration
put.description: Add a vCenter for monitoring with specified application monitoring configuration

parameters.name: appMonitoringUrl
parameters.description: Url of the application monitoring

parameters.name: vcUrl
parameters.description: The url of the vCenter to be monitored

200.description: The vCenter is added to the configuration successfully

put.summary: UnSubscribe to Application Monitoring by downloading bootstrap bundle containing all required scripts for Unbootstraping ARC Agent from Endpoints.
put.description:
Bootstrap Bundle primarily contains client certificates, Grains File containing configuration of
ARC Agent Components (ucp-minion, salt-minion, Telegraf) and seeder script.

parameters.name: vmIp
parameters.description: The IP of Virtual Machine

parameters.name: ostype
parameters.description: The OS type, e.g. Windows, Unix

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: Flag that indicates whether to clean all traces of application monitoring agent from vrops and ARC or not

parameters.name: collectorIP_or_GroupNm
parameters.description: IP of the Collector or Collector Group VirtualIP or Collector Group Name

200.description: The bootstrap bundle binary zip file

put.summary: Subscribe to Application Monitoring by downloading bootstrap bundle containing all required scripts for bootstraping ARC Agent from Endpoints
put.description:
Bootstrap Bundle primarily contains client certificates, Grains File containing configuration of
ARC Agent Components (ucp-minion, salt-minion, Telegraf) and seeder script.
This API also preseeds salt minion keys in ARC.
In SAAS, it also creates adapter if adapter does not exist.

parameters.name: ostype
parameters.description: The OS type, e.g. Windows, Unix

parameters.name: vmIp
parameters.description: The IP of Virtual Machine or UUID of Endpoint

parameters.name: collectorIP_or_GroupNm
parameters.description: IP of the Collector or Collector Group VirtualIP or Collector Group Name

200.description: The bootstrap bundle binary zip file

put.summary: Update an existing Notification Plugin Instance
put.description:
Before updating a notification plugin, the plugin should be disabled. During the update process
if there are any untrusted certificates returned by the server the patchAlertPlugin call should be made.
Once the update is successfully completed, use modifyAlertPluginState API to enable the plugin.

requestBody.description: The NotificationPlugin to create, including plugin type and configuration details

post.summary: Create a new Notification Plugin Instance
post.description:
If the Notification plugin is a standard email plugin, then any untrusted certificates from the secure SMTP server
is returned as part of the response, the api client should invoke a PATCH call to store these certificates.
By default the notification plugin is in disabled state after being created.
Use the modifyAlertPluginState API to enable the plugin.

get.summary: Get collection of Alert Definitions matching the search criteria specified
get.description: If the search criteria is not satisfied, the API will return all Alert Definitions

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: The Adapter Kind key of the Alert Definitions to search for

schema.description: The Adapter Kind key of the Alert Definitions to search for

parameters.name: resourceKind
parameters.description: The Resource Kind key of the Alert Definitions to search for

schema.description: The Resource Kind key of the Alert Definitions to search for

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The identifier(s) of the Alert Definitions to search for.
Do not specify adapterKind or resourceKind if searching by the identifier

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:alert-definition>
        <ops:id>f080147f-c60d-4f08-8801-b602e2931b6f</ops:id>
        <ops:name>High CPU/Memory Usage</ops:name>
        <ops:description>CPU or MEM usage too High</ops:description>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>HostSystem</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:waitCycles>3</ops:waitCycles>
        <ops:cancelCycles>2</ops:cancelCycles>
        <ops:type>0</ops:type>
        <ops:subType>0</ops:subType>
        <ops:states>
            <ops:state severity="WARNING">
                <ops:composite-symptom-set>
                    <ops:operator>AND</ops:operator>
                    <ops:symptom-sets>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom5</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>!Symptom6</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:alertConditions>
                                <ops:alertCondition>
                                    <ops:waitCycles>5</ops:waitCycles>
                                    <ops:cancelCycles>5</ops:cancelCycles>
                                    <ops:severity>CRITICAL</ops:severity>
                                    <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                                        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                                        <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                                        <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                                        <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                                        <ops:targetKey>cpu|availablemhz</ops:targetKey>
                                        <ops:thresholdType>STATKEY</ops:thresholdType>
                                    </ops:condition>
                                </ops:alertCondition>
                            </ops:alertConditions>
                            <ops:relation>DESCENDANT</ops:relation>
                            <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKindKey>Datastore</ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>AND</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom100</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom101</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:relation>SELF</ops:relation>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>OR</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                    </ops:symptom-sets>
                </ops:composite-symptom-set>
                <ops:impact>
                    <ops:impactType>BADGE</ops:impactType>
                    <ops:detail>health</ops:detail>
                </ops:impact>
            </ops:state>
        </ops:states>
        <ops:forVCDTenants>false</ops:forVCDTenants>
    </ops:alert-definition>
    <ops:alert-definition>
        <ops:id>AlertDefinition-147ff080-8801-c60d-4f08-931b6fb602e2</ops:id>
        <ops:name>MyAlertDefinition</ops:name>
        <ops:description>An Alert Definition associated with ESX host</ops:description>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>HostSystem</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:waitCycles>3</ops:waitCycles>
        <ops:cancelCycles>2</ops:cancelCycles>
        <ops:type>0</ops:type>
        <ops:subType>0</ops:subType>
        <ops:states>
            <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
                <ops:symptom-set>
                    <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                        <ops:symptomDefinitionId>SymptomDefinition-5</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                    </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                    <ops:relation>SELF</ops:relation>
                    <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                </ops:symptom-set>
                <ops:impact>
                    <ops:impactType>BADGE</ops:impactType>
                    <ops:detail>Health</ops:detail>
                </ops:impact>
            </ops:state>
        </ops:states>
        <ops:forVCDTenants>false</ops:forVCDTenants>
    </ops:alert-definition>
</ops:alert-definitions>

                        <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                        <ops:symptomSetOperator>AND</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                    </ops:symptom-set>
                    <ops:symptom-set>
                        <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>

                            <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom101</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                        </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                        <ops:relation>SELF</ops:relation>
                        <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                        <ops:symptomSetOperator>OR</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                    </ops:symptom-set>
                </ops:symptom-sets>
            </ops:composite-symptom-set>
            <ops:impact>
                <ops:impactType>BADGE</ops:impactType>

            </ops:impact>
        </ops:state>

</ops:alert-definition>

                        <ops:relation>DESCENDANT</ops:relation>

Pass the not-required parameters to indicate whether need to return default value or null in a result for identifiers that have no/null value in request body and continue instantiating adapter even if no connection is established.
In case of VCenter adapter instance, UI/license extension registration should be run separately.

parameters.name: extractIdentifierDefaults

Indicates whether need to return default value or null in a result for
identifiers that have no/null value in request body

patch.description:
This is typically used to indicate VMware Cloud Foundation operations that the certificates presented by
the (data source for which the adapter instance) was created are indeed valid
certificates.
Typically the response of the POST /api/adapters API in its entirety needs to be
sent as request body when making this PATCH /api/adapters API invocation.
NOTE: In case the provided certificate list is empty, the adapter instance self-signed certificates will be reset.

                <ops:value>true</ops:value>
            </ops:resourceIdentifier>
            <ops:resourceIdentifier>
                <ops:identifierType name="VCURL" dataType="STRING" isPartOfUniqueness="true"/>
                <ops:value>https://ip/sdk</ops:value>

get.summary: Look up stats of a group of objects with the resource ids
get.description: NOTE: Unless a time range is explicitly set, the method returns statKeys which have received data in the last 24 hours.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched

parameters.name: statKey
parameters.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

schema.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

parameters.name: rollUpType
parameters.description: Rollup Type requested by the user (e.g.: AVG, MIN, MAX etc.)

schema.description: Represents the type of Rollup required for the stats data

parameters.name: intervalType
parameters.description: Interval type requested by the user (e.g: Days, Weeks, Months etc.)

schema.description: The Interval types

parameters.name: intervalQuantifier
parameters.description: Number for the interval type

schema.description: Number for the interval type

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

schema.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

parameters.name: latestMaxSamples
parameters.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

schema.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

parameters.name: metrics
parameters.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

schema.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

parameters.name: currentOnly
parameters.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

schema.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

parameters.name: wrapStatValues
parameters.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

schema.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Identifier of the resource object

200.description: Object, containing a map of List of Stat objects keyed by each resource UUID

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:stats-of-resources xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:stats-of-resource>
        <ops:resourceId>c28f14c0-ef58-4792-9bde-ce7ec10e52da</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:stat-list>
            <ops:stat>
                <ops:timestamps>1744473856413 1744473866413</ops:timestamps>
                <ops:statKey>
                    <ops:key>stat|key1</ops:key>
                </ops:statKey>
                <ops:rollUpType>NONE</ops:rollUpType>
                <ops:intervalUnit>
                    <ops:quantifier>5</ops:quantifier>
                    <ops:intervalType>MINUTES</ops:intervalType>
                </ops:intervalUnit>
                <ops:data>1.0 2.0</ops:data>
                <ops:dtTimestamps>1744473856413 1744473866413 1744473876413</ops:dtTimestamps>
                <ops:minThresholdData>0.5 1.0</ops:minThresholdData>
                <ops:maxThresholdData>1.5 3.0</ops:maxThresholdData>
                <ops:smoothValues>1.5 1.5</ops:smoothValues>
            </ops:stat>
        </ops:stat-list>
    </ops:stats-of-resource>
</ops:stats-of-resources>

post.summary: Add Stats to a Resource
post.description: In this case, the adapter instance used for pushing data will default to vRealizeOpsMgrAPI. It is recommended (though not required) to use this API when the resource was created using the API POST /api/resources/{id}/adapters/{adapterInstanceId}. Otherwise an additional adapter instance might be created.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Identifier of the resource object

parameters.name: disableAnalyticsProcessing
parameters.description: Whether the current data set goes through Analytics processing (Threshold checking, Alert generation) or not. Defaults to false. But when set to true will result in no evaluation of Alerts but the data gets stored.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:stat-contents xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        <ops:timestamps>1119844280693</ops:timestamps>
        <ops:timestamps>1119844280713</ops:timestamps>
        <ops:data>0.2</ops:data>
        <ops:data>0.3</ops:data>
        <ops:data>0.4</ops:data>
    </ops:stat-content>
    <ops:stat-content statKey="mem|overload">
        <ops:timestamps>1119844280663</ops:timestamps>

post.summary: Add Properties to a Resource
post.description:
In this case, the adapter instance used for pushing data will default to SuiteAPI. It is recommended (though not required) to use this API when the resource was created using the API
POST /api/resources/{id}/adapters/{adapterInstanceId}. Otherwise an additional adapter instance might be created.
Note: Those properties that are specified with old timestamp will be published with current time. As a result, only one of those properties will appear in vROps (e.g. the latest one will overwrite all previous ones).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the resource

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:property-contents xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    <ops:property-content statKey="config|num|processes">

        <ops:data>93.0</ops:data>
    </ops:property-content>
</ops:property-contents>

get.summary: Get stats for one or more Resources using the specific query spec
get.description:
This is a GET endpoint and the query parameters control the data that is retrieved.
If the number of Resources for which one needs to query data is large, it is recommended to
use the POST /api/stats/query endpoint to avoid the URL length limitations.
Takes the form:
api/resources/stats?resourceId=UUID1 OR
api/resources/stats?resourceId=UUID2&statKey=cpu|usagemhz&rollUpType=&begin=&end=

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Array of Resource ids for which the stats should be fetched

parameters.name: statKey
parameters.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned

parameters.name: begin
parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

schema.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

parameters.name: rollUpType
parameters.description: Rollup Type requested by the user (e.g.: AVG, MIN, MAX etc.)

schema.description: Represents the type of Rollup required for the stats data

parameters.name: intervalType
parameters.description: Interval type requested by the user (e.g: Days, Weeks, Months etc.)

schema.description: The Interval types

parameters.name: intervalQuantifier
parameters.description: Number for the interval type

schema.description: Number for the interval type

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

schema.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

parameters.name: latestMaxSamples
parameters.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

schema.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

parameters.name: metrics
parameters.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

schema.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

parameters.name: currentOnly
parameters.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

schema.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

parameters.name: wrapStatValues
parameters.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

schema.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

post.summary: Create a new Resource in the system associated with an existing adapter instance
post.description:
The API will create the missing Adapter Kind and Resource Kind
contained within the ResourceKey of the Resource if they do not exist.
The API will return an error if the adapter instance specified does not exist.
Additional implementation notes:

  • When creating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique and required
    are not specified, the API would return an error with HTTP status code of 500
    and an error message indicating the set of missing Resource Identifiers.

  • When creating a Resource, if the Resource Identifiers that are unique but not required
    are not specified, the Resource is created where the uniquely identifying Resource Identifiers
    that were not specified will have their value as an empty string.

<ops:report xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

get.summary: Get all available policies.
get.description: Returns all available policies.

parameters.name: activesOnly
parameters.description: True to get only active policies (the ones that have priority, and default policy).

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:policy-summaries-collection xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:policySummaries>
        <ops:policySummary id="0e1b962c-b9cd-405b-91d0-3d00f7a8dc8f" name="XYZ Production Policy">
            <ops:defaultPolicy>false</ops:defaultPolicy>
        </ops:policySummary>
        <ops:policySummary id="d769c1c9-7607-4cde-b749-75b84d741dd1" name="CPU Overcommit Policy">
            <ops:defaultPolicy>false</ops:defaultPolicy>
        </ops:policySummary>
    </ops:policySummaries>
</ops:policy-summaries-collection>

post.summary: Create a new policy.
post.description: Create a new policy.

requestBody.description: The Policy object which can be used to create the policy

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:policy name="XYZ Production Policy" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:description>=Production Policy Description</ops:description>
    <ops:parentPolicy>3b76afea-2ffa-49cb-800f-054f3c1ed1a3</ops:parentPolicy>
</ops:policy>

400.description: An invalid request body was specified

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

post.summary: Import policy content from an archive of ZIP format
post.description: Imports policy content from an archive of ZIP format.

parameters.name: forceImport
parameters.description: True to overwrite the existing policies, false to skip importing when there is a conflict

policy.description: An archive of policy content to be imported

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:policy-update-outcome xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    <ops:updatedPolicies>
        <ops:updatedPolicy id="2aa2d2ef-78e2-4df0-9500-a6de0d51aa72" name="RAM Allocation Policy">
            <ops:defaultPolicy>false</ops:defaultPolicy>
        </ops:updatedPolicy>
    </ops:updatedPolicies>
    <ops:skippedPolicies>

            <ops:defaultPolicy>false</ops:defaultPolicy>
        </ops:skippedPolicy>
    </ops:skippedPolicies>
</ops:policy-update-outcome>

post.summary: Test VCF domain, by provided domain update model, VCF id and domain id
post.description: NOTE: This operation will only work for configured domains.

parameters.name: integrationId
parameters.description: The identifier of the VCF integration in UUID format.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: The identifier of the VCF's domain in UUID format.

400.description: If update 'VCF domain' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why the test failed. In case if connection testing failed due to certificates, untrusted certificates will be returned for acceptance.

200.description: Provided update model passed the test

404.description: If no VCF domain was found by provided domain id, or no VCF was found by provided VCF id

post.summary: Test provided VCF integration
post.description: In case if certificate is needed, will return untrusted certificate for acceptance in response

200.description: Provided VCF integration, successfully passed the test

400.description: If test 'VCF integration' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why the test failed. In case if connection testing failed due to certificates, untrusted certificates will be returned for acceptance.

422.description: If test 'vCenter integration' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why the test failed.

post.summary: Test provided vCenter integration
post.description: In case if certificate is needed, will return untrusted certificate for acceptance in response

400.description: If test 'vCenter integration' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why the test failed. In case if connection testing failed due to certificates, untrusted certificates will be returned for acceptance.

422.description: If test 'vCenter integration' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why the test failed.

200.description: Provided vCenter integration, successfully passed the test

post.summary: Push one or more Events into the system with associating an adapter kind as the event source

If the adapter kind specified is not present in the system, it will be created dynamically. However,
if the adapter kind specified does already exist, then it must be of OPENAPI adapter kind type.
Also the API sanitizes the Push Adapter Kind key by removing invalid characters (e.g.: Embedded HTML & JS)

<ops:events xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:event eventType="NOTIFICATION" resourceId="e3029775-cf54-432f-b31f-470771ead423" startTimeUTC="946685500000" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>I have a notification for you.</ops:message>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="RESOURCE_DOWN" resourceId="ff2b9f17-ce3b-4edb-9e05-0e191e4bf47d" startTimeUTC="946685500000" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>There is another problem somewhere far far away in the Environment.</ops:message>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="SYSTEM_DEGRADATION" resourceId="c1955c95-cf9e-4fa7-877c-b6e2716f272f" startTimeUTC="946685500000" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="WARNING" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>What is this problem with the system</ops:message>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="CHANGE" resourceId="ac68f35f-5209-4bc1-adb4-78b554e39268" startTimeUTC="946685500000" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" managedExternally="false">
        <ops:message>A change will do you good</ops:message>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="HARD_THRESHOLD" resourceId="e0622b33-cca6-4624-a1c7-4e93cd4f541a" startTimeUTC="946685520002" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" htType="ABOVE" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>metric is above expected threshold</ops:message>
        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
        <ops:value>90.0</ops:value>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="HARD_THRESHOLD" resourceId="d395aa61-7c1c-41cf-95c5-fdb893644fd3" startTimeUTC="946685530003" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" htType="BELOW" keyIndicator="true" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>metric is below expected threshold</ops:message>
        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
        <ops:value>20.0</ops:value>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="HARD_THRESHOLD" resourceId="009f7b85-dcd9-481c-a447-ac047d0e405e" startTimeUTC="946685540004" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" htType="EQUAL" managedExternally="false">
        <ops:message>metric is not equal to expected target 5000</ops:message>
        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
        <ops:value>12345.0</ops:value>
    </ops:event>
    <ops:event eventType="HARD_THRESHOLD" resourceId="9f216b16-1734-4aa8-89e6-4dfa630dd467" startTimeUTC="946685550005" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" htType="NOT_EQUAL" managedExternally="true">
        <ops:message>metric is equal to abnormal value</ops:message>
        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
        <ops:value>3.14</ops:value>
    </ops:event>
</ops:events>

post.summary: Push a single Event into the system with associating an adapter kind as the event source
post.description:
If the adapter kind specified is not present in the system, it will be created dynamically. However,
if the adapter kind specified does already exist, then it must be of OPENAPI adapter kind type.
Also the API sanitizes the Push Adapter Kind key by removing invalid characters (e.g.: Embedded HTML & JS)

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: The adapter kind key of the adapter source

application/json.example:
{
  "eventType" : "NOTIFICATION",
  "resourceId" : "6fe466cf-c588-433d-a5dc-2d23a3d4bcb0",
  "startTimeUTC" : 946685500000,
  "cancelTimeUTC" : 0,
  "message" : "There is a problem somewhere nearby.",
  "severity" : "NONE",
  "managedExternally" : true
}

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:event eventType="NOTIFICATION" resourceId="bbd651e5-712e-47b6-845c-7f6c5e54fbd7" startTimeUTC="946685500000" cancelTimeUTC="0" severity="NONE" managedExternally="true" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:message>There is a problem somewhere nearby.</ops:message>
</ops:event>

post.summary: Import content for current user from uploaded file
post.description:
The "Content-Type" of the request is multipart/form-data. If the force option is set to true, content will be overwritten. By default the flag is true.
NOTE: If the content file contains integration accounts, then upon completion of the import process, it is recommended to check them. Some collectors or collector groups may have become unbound from the accounts they serve and should be reconnected. Additionally, you will need to re-accept all untrusted certificates manually. Finally ensure that all your adapter instances are started as some of these may not have been restarted automatically.

parameters.name: EncryptionPassword
parameters.description: Key for decrypting sensitive information

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: True to force solution content overwrite

202.description: The import of content has been accepted

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:content-import id="bb1f4c64-b1e9-4a3f-a051-790d926342d2" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:fileName>contentFile</ops:fileName>
    <ops:force>false</ops:force>
    <ops:links>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/content/operations/import" rel="RELATED" name="ImportStatusCheckURL">
            <ops:description>Status of the last import operation</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
    </ops:links>
</ops:content-import>

post.summary: Export content for current user
post.description: Export content for current user.  This takes scope of the export.  The content types that need to be exported can be specified as a list.

parameters.name: EncryptionPassword
parameters.description: Key for encrypting sensitive information

requestBody.description: Export Details of Type and Segregation

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:content-export xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:scope>CUSTOM</ops:scope>
    <ops:contentTypes>POLICIES OUTBOUND_SETTINGS INTEGRATIONS</ops:contentTypes>
</ops:content-export>

202.description: The export of content has been accepted

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:content-export xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:scope>CUSTOM</ops:scope>
    <ops:contentTypes>POLICIES OUTBOUND_SETTINGS INTEGRATIONS</ops:contentTypes>
    <ops:links>
        <ops:link href="/suite-api/api/content/operations/export" rel="RELATED" name="ExportStatusCheckURL">
            <ops:description>Status of the last export operation</ops:description>
        </ops:link>
    </ops:links>
</ops:content-export>

get.summary: Get the Chargeback Reports based on the Report Query Spec provided
get.description: If the query spec is not provided, all Chargeback Reports present in the system are returned.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Reports name with which the filtering can be done

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subjects with which the filtering can be done

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status of the report

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Resources on which the reports are generated

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

200.description: The Collection of Chargeback Reports matching the specified search critiera

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:reports xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:report>
        <ops:description>Cluster Capacity Risk Forecast Report</ops:description>
        <ops:subject></ops:subject>
        <ops:owner>admin</ops:owner>
        <ops:status>Completed</ops:status>

post.summary: Acquire a token to perform REST API calls
post.description:
Performing a POST /api/auth/token/acquire would yield a response object that includes token and its validity.
HTTP Status 401 will be sent back if the authentication operation has failed.
NOTE: The validity of the acquired token is extended after each call and is set to 6 hours from the last call.

requestBody.description: The UsernamePassword object that encapsulates username, password and optionally the name of an Auth Source as specified by the user

</ops:username-password>

post.summary: Search users for an auth source
post.description:
The auth source can be SSO/LDAP/AD/VIDM.
For the LDAP/AD auth source, the request should contain the name of the user.
For the SSO/VIDM auth source, request should contain the name of the user and the SSO/VIDM domain.
Username can contain wildcards. Maximum of 25 users are returned for SSO auth source.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the auth source

requestBody.description:
The query object for user search
Notes Examples for LDAP/SSO users

200.description:
Users matching the request query
Notes Examples for LDAP/SSO users

post.summary: Search user-groups for an auth source
post.description:
The auth source can be SSO/VIDM/LDAP/AD.
For the LDAP/AD auth source, the request should contain the name of the group.
For the SSO/VIDM auth source, request should contain the name of the group and the SSO/VIDM domain.
User-group's name can contain wildcards. Maximum of 25 user-groups are returned for SSO auth source.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the auth source

requestBody.description:
The query object for user group search
Notes Examples for LDAP/SSO user groups

200.description:
User-groups matching the request query
Notes Examples for LDAP/SSO user groups

post.summary: Install agent on resource
post.description:
Install app monitoring agent on specified resource.
vCenters mapping should be present for this API to work.

Correct configuration for vCenters mapping -
vCenter is mapped to individual CP or CP part of non HA collector group.
vCenter is mapped to HA enabled collector group.

vCenters mapping APIs -
Create vCenters mappings API POST /api/applications/vccpmappings.
Delete vCenters mapping API DELETE /api/applications/vccpmappings.

requestBody.description: Credentials of resources required to install the agent

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:resource-credentials retainTelegrafConf="true" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        <ops:resourceCredential resourceId="1c8ee358-ca2a-4c56-86e0-1b7daa3fd4de" username="vm_username1" password="vm_password1">
            <ops:addRuntimeUser>true</ops:addRuntimeUser>
        </ops:resourceCredential>
        <ops:resourceCredential resourceId="1bfe3f5e-fab8-4ba8-b73b-7be591339a13" username="vm_username2" password="vm_password2">
            <ops:addRuntimeUser>true</ops:addRuntimeUser>
        </ops:resourceCredential>
    </ops:resourceCredentials>
</ops:resource-credentials>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>

        <ops:taskStatus taskID="task_id_1">
            <ops:resources>
                <ops:resource>vm_id_1</ops:resource>
                <ops:resource>vm_id_2</ops:resource>
