        </ops:taskStatus>
        <ops:taskStatus taskID="task_id_2">
            <ops:resources>
                <ops:resource>vm_id_3</ops:resource>

            </ops:resources>
        </ops:taskStatus>
    </ops:taskStatuses>
</ops:agent-task-statuses>

post.summary: Perform operations on one or more Alerts
post.description:
Modify multiple Alerts by looking them up using their identifiers and performing one of the following actions - Suspend, Cancel,Take Ownership, Release Ownership
Examples:
POST /api/alerts?action=suspend&minutes=1
POST /api/alerts?action=cancel
POST /api/alerts?action=takeownership
POST /api/alerts?action=releaseownership
POST /api/alerts?action=assignownership

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action to take

parameters.name: minutes
parameters.description: The number of minutes to suspend an alert. Required when action is Suspend, otherwise ignored.

parameters.name: userAccountID
parameters.description: User Account identifier. Optional request parameter that is applicable when action is <b><i>assignownership<b><i>.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

post.summary: Query for Alerts using one or more criteria specified in the Query Spec

        <ops:regex>\\S+-BNA-\\S+</ops:regex>
        <ops:regex xsi:nil="true"/>
        <ops:adapterKind>VMWARE</ops:adapterKind>
        <ops:resourceKind>HostSystem</ops:resourceKind>
        <ops:resourceId>0270695a-5a31-47cf-87de-f01680911ea1</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:statKeyInclusive>true</ops:statKeyInclusive>
    </ops:resource-query>
    <ops:activeOnly>true</ops:activeOnly>
    <ops:alertCriticality>CRITICAL IMMEDIATE WARNING INFORMATION</ops:alertCriticality>
    <ops:alertStatus></ops:alertStatus>

    <ops:startTimeRange>

        <ops:endTime>1744483855</ops:endTime>
    </ops:startTimeRange>
    <ops:includeChildrenResources>true</ops:includeChildrenResources>
    <ops:extractOwnerName>false</ops:extractOwnerName>
</ops:alert-query>

post.summary: Query for Alert groups using one or more criteria specified in the Query Spec
post.description: Example: POST /api/alerts/group/{groupingCondition}/query

parameters.name: groupingCondition
parameters.description: The condition to be used for grouping

schema.description: Indicates the alert grouping condition

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: Adapter kind to be used for grouping alerts by SCOPE

parameters.name: resourceKind
parameters.description: Resource kind to be used for grouping alerts by SCOPE

parameters.name: resourceNameFilter
parameters.description: Filter based on alert resource name (Used only in SCOPE flow)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-query compositeOperator="AND" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:alertId></ops:alertId>
    <ops:resource-query>
        <ops:name>Windows2017VM</ops:name>
        <ops:name>Windows2018VM</ops:name>
        <ops:regex>\\S+-BNA-\\S+</ops:regex>
        <ops:regex xsi:nil="true"/>
        <ops:adapterKind>VMWARE</ops:adapterKind>
        <ops:resourceKind>HostSystem</ops:resourceKind>
        <ops:resourceId>4bbc79e4-80cb-4d6d-ab74-45ba227cfd62</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:statKeyInclusive>true</ops:statKeyInclusive>
    </ops:resource-query>
    <ops:activeOnly>true</ops:activeOnly>
    <ops:alertCriticality>CRITICAL IMMEDIATE WARNING INFORMATION</ops:alertCriticality>
    <ops:alertStatus></ops:alertStatus>
    <ops:alertImpact></ops:alertImpact>
    <ops:alertControlState></ops:alertControlState>
    <ops:startTimeRange>
        <ops:startTime>1744473856</ops:startTime>
        <ops:endTime>1744483856</ops:endTime>
    </ops:startTimeRange>
    <ops:includeChildrenResources>true</ops:includeChildrenResources>
    <ops:extractOwnerName>false</ops:extractOwnerName>
</ops:alert-query>

500.description: Error occurred while retrieving the AlertGroups

200.description: AlertGroups of alerts matching the specified Query Spec and grouped using specified grouping-condition(Collection can be empty)

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-groups xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:alert-group>
        <ops:groupId>A-id</ops:groupId>
        <ops:groupName>A</ops:groupName>
        <ops:alertCount>5</ops:alertCount>

    <ops:alert-group>

        <ops:groupName>B</ops:groupName>
        <ops:alertCount>5</ops:alertCount>
        <ops:maxCriticalityLevel>3</ops:maxCriticalityLevel>
    </ops:alert-group>
</ops:alert-groups>

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-definition-query xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    </ops:adapterKinds>
    <ops:resourceKinds>

    </ops:resourceKinds>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:alert-definitions xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        <ops:id>f080147f-c60d-4f08-8801-b602e2931b6f</ops:id>
        <ops:name>High CPU/Memory Usage</ops:name>
        <ops:description>CPU or MEM usage too High</ops:description>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:resourceKindKey>HostSystem</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:waitCycles>3</ops:waitCycles>
        <ops:cancelCycles>2</ops:cancelCycles>
        <ops:type>0</ops:type>
        <ops:subType>0</ops:subType>
        <ops:states>
            <ops:state severity="WARNING">
                <ops:composite-symptom-set>
                    <ops:operator>AND</ops:operator>
                    <ops:symptom-sets>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom5</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>!Symptom6</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:alertConditions>
                                <ops:alertCondition>
                                    <ops:waitCycles>5</ops:waitCycles>
                                    <ops:cancelCycles>5</ops:cancelCycles>
                                    <ops:severity>CRITICAL</ops:severity>
                                    <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
                                        <ops:key>cpu|demandmhz</ops:key>
                                        <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
                                        <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
                                        <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
                                        <ops:targetKey>cpu|availablemhz</ops:targetKey>
                                        <ops:thresholdType>STATKEY</ops:thresholdType>
                                    </ops:condition>
                                </ops:alertCondition>
                            </ops:alertConditions>
                            <ops:relation>DESCENDANT</ops:relation>
                            <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
                            <ops:resourceKindKey>Datastore</ops:resourceKindKey>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>AND</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                        <ops:symptom-set>
                            <ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom100</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                                <ops:symptomDefinitionId>Symptom101</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
                            </ops:symptomDefinitionIds>
                            <ops:relation>SELF</ops:relation>
                            <ops:aggregation>ALL</ops:aggregation>
                            <ops:symptomSetOperator>OR</ops:symptomSetOperator>
                        </ops:symptom-set>
                    </ops:symptom-sets>
                </ops:composite-symptom-set>
                <ops:impact>
                    <ops:impactType>BADGE</ops:impactType>
                    <ops:detail>health</ops:detail>
                </ops:impact>
            </ops:state>
        </ops:states>
        <ops:forVCDTenants>false</ops:forVCDTenants>

post.summary: Test Adapter Instance connection

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:create-adapter-instance xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        <ops:resourceIdentifier name="AUTODISCOVERY">true</ops:resourceIdentifier>
        <ops:resourceIdentifier name="PROCESSCHANGEEVENTS">true</ops:resourceIdentifier>
        <ops:resourceIdentifier name="VCURL">VC_IP_Address</ops:resourceIdentifier>
    </ops:resourceIdentifiers>
    <ops:credential>
        <ops:name>Principal Credential</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:credentialKindKey>PRINCIPALCREDENTIAL</ops:credentialKindKey>

patch.description:
This is typically used to indicate VMware Cloud Foundation operations that the certificates presented by
the (data source for which the adapter instance) was tested are indeed valid
certificates.
Typically the response of the POST /api/adapters API in its entirety needs to be
sent as request body when making this PATCH /api/adapters API invocation.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:adapter-instance id="26b5cd94-85b9-4e73-97dd-2d797a88e322" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        <ops:name>VC Adapter Instance</ops:name>

        <ops:resourceKindKey>VMwareAdapter Instance</ops:resourceKindKey>
        <ops:resourceIdentifiers/>
    </ops:resourceKey>
    <ops:description>A vCenter Adapter Instance</ops:description>
    <ops:collectorId>1</ops:collectorId>
    <ops:credentialInstanceId>393e14d0-310a-4f69-8b16-fa49da7e8524</ops:credentialInstanceId>
    <ops:adapter-certificates>
        <ops:adapter-certificate>
            <ops:thumbprint>e797bedf-74b0-4681-a74a-de8f524d6cb1</ops:thumbprint>
            <ops:certificateDetails>Issued by VMware, Inc. Expires: 01/01/2020</ops:certificateDetails>
        </ops:adapter-certificate>
    </ops:adapter-certificates>
</ops:adapter-instance>

400.description: If Patch 'adapter instance connection' failed. Typically the service will return extra information as to why update failed.

post.summary: Perform an Action on one or more resources
post.description:
Performing a POST /api/actions/{id} will attempt to trigger the
action with specified id. It is considered a best practice to use the actionExecution field from the
POST /api/action/{id}/query that has been previously executed to populate
the body of the request. Please note that additional values may be required in order to make
the populateAction response body valid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id for the action to be executed (can be retrieved via: GET /api/actiondefinitions)

requestBody.description: Describe instance data about performing an action.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-execution contextId="PowerOffVM" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:contextResourceId>7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8</ops:contextResourceId>
    <ops:parameterGroup resourceId="7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8"/>
</ops:action-execution>

200.description: List of Task Ids for the action run.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:str-values xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:values>b028aa9b-ffd7-4ae6-b9dc-f27aa1865e59</ops:values>
</ops:str-values>

post.summary: Populate the information needed to run an action
post.description:
Performing a POST /api/actions/{id}/query would yield a response object that
includes the resources that the action would apply to with the populated values of each action
context field for the resource. The response contains information about the parameters to the
action's method. It also includes a sample input that would be used for running the action with
POST /api/actions/{id} if you chose to run it on all applicable resources
and not change the default input values.

The json result inside actionExecution can be used directly as input perform the action. In the xml
result, the xml element ops:action-execution will need namespace binding added to it in order to use it
in the request.
xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Id for the action to be populate.

requestBody.description: The additional information needed to populate the action.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-population xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:contextResourceId>c5af764a-d4d5-4258-829c-8acb0604e4c7</ops:contextResourceId>
</ops:action-population>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:populated-action id="VMWARE-Power Off VM" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:actionContext id="Power Off VM">
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field id="Name" name="VM Name" component="textfield" input="false" displayOrder="0" hidden="false">
                <ops:expressionResults>
                    <ops:expressionResult>MyVMName</ops:expressionResult>
                </ops:expressionResults>
            </ops:field>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:actionContext>
    <ops:methodParameters>
        <ops:methodParameter id="mOR" dataType="String" isRequired="true" isList="false"/>
    </ops:methodParameters>
    <ops:action-execution contextId="PowerOffVM">
        <ops:contextResourceId>7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8</ops:contextResourceId>
        <ops:parameterGroup resourceId="7e780215-da07-4da1-9167-cd6892dcfdd8"/>
    </ops:action-execution>
</ops:populated-action>

get.summary: Get policy settings for the specified identifier.
get.description:
Get the policy settings for the specified identifier.
NOTE: Currently, only one resource kind is supported.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Policy identifier to get

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Types of policy settings to retrieve.

items.description: Indicates policy settings type

parameters.name: includeInherited
parameters.description: True to obtain complete configuration, included inherited settings from parent policies

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: The adapter kind identifier

parameters.name: resourceKind
parameters.description: The resource kind identifiers

200.description: The policy settings for the specified identifier.

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:policy-settings xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:vcPricingSettings>
        <ops:pricingPolicyStatus>
            <ops:enabled>false</ops:enabled>
        </ops:pricingPolicyStatus>
    </ops:vcPricingSettings>
</ops:policy-settings>

404.description: There is no policy for the specified identifier.

delete.summary: Make provided settings in the policy inherited from parent policy.
delete.description:
Make provided settings in the policy inherited from parent policy.
NOTE: Currently, only one resource kind is supported.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Policy identifier

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Types of policy settings to delete.

items.description: Indicates policy settings type

parameters.name: adapterKind
parameters.description: The adapter kind identifier

parameters.name: resourceKind
parameters.description: The resource kind identifiers

404.description: The policy is invalid/non-existent

204.description: Successfully made policy settings as inherited.

patch.summary: Update only provided settings in the policy.
patch.description: Update only provided settings in the policy.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Policy identifier to update

requestBody.description: Only the specified fields in the settings will be applied to the provided policy

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:policy-settings xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:vcPricingSettings>
        <ops:pricingPolicyStatus>
            <ops:enabled>false</ops:enabled>
        </ops:pricingPolicyStatus>
    </ops:vcPricingSettings>
</ops:policy-settings>

400.description: An invalid request body was specified

204.description: Policy settings was updated.

get.summary: Look up a list of task objects' statuses
get.description:
In general, to get the status of any long running operations, the operation
must have been initiated through the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations REST API.
This API endpoint returns the details of the long running tasks that were submitted via the API only.
Furthermore, the list of tasks that this API returns includes tasks for long running operations such
as Adapter Installation, Describe etc. This API does not return the tasks created when any
Remediation Actions are executed in the system either via the UI or via the API.

parameters.name: taskState
parameters.description: States of the tasks

schema.description: States of the tasks

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: The task ids

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:task-statuses xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:task-status>
        <ops:taskId>01e0d1e6-77f7-477d-8918-f401e922584d</ops:taskId>
        <ops:statusMessage>Distributed Task Execution for ADAPTER_INSTALL</ops:statusMessage>
        <ops:taskState>RUNNING</ops:taskState>
        <ops:createdTime>0</ops:createdTime>
        <ops:lastUpdateTime>1744473856274</ops:lastUpdateTime>
        <ops:errorMessages/>
    </ops:task-status>
</ops:task-statuses>

get.summary: Look up a task status with the given identifier
get.description:
In general, to get the status of any long running operations, the operation
must have been initiated through the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations REST API.
This API returns the status of a specific task that was initiated via the REST API
with the exception of tasks that get created when any Remediation Action is performed
via the api/actions endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the task

404.description: No Task is found with the specified identifier

200.description: Task status with the specified identifier

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:task-status xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:taskId>4cb844d6-ee58-4dd7-bbb5-6abaac798edc</ops:taskId>
    <ops:statusMessage>Distributed Task Execution for ADAPTER_INSTALL</ops:statusMessage>
    <ops:taskState>RUNNING</ops:taskState>
    <ops:createdTime>0</ops:createdTime>
    <ops:lastUpdateTime>1744473856280</ops:lastUpdateTime>
    <ops:errorMessages/>
</ops:task-status>

get.summary: Look up all Symptoms in the system
get.description:
Optionally these Symptoms can be looked up for a specific set of Resources and/or
can include Symptoms that are no longer active.
The list of Symptoms returned is a collection that supports pagination.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Array of resource identifiers for which Symptoms need to be looked up

parameters.name: activeOnly
parameters.description: Boolean that determines whether the results should include inactive Symptoms

parameters.name: includeAlarmInfo
parameters.description: Boolean that controls the retrieval of alarm information of the symptom

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

200.description: List of Symptoms in the system

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptoms xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:symptom id="31eeaeec-82d5-4037-a59b-efed2e7c8e3a" kpi="false">
        <ops:resourceId>50000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:startTimeUTC>1744470256299</ops:startTimeUTC>
        <ops:updateTimeUTC>1744470256299</ops:updateTimeUTC>
        <ops:cancelTimeUTC>0</ops:cancelTimeUTC>
        <ops:symptomCriticality>INFORMATION</ops:symptomCriticality>
        <ops:symptomDefinitionId>SymptomDefinition-1</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
        <ops:message>symptom message</ops:message>
        <ops:faultDevices/>
    </ops:symptom>
    <ops:symptom id="18589dea-9999-1234-abba-f1ee73004dac" kpi="false">
        <ops:resourceId>50000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005</ops:resourceId>
        <ops:startTimeUTC>1744470256299</ops:startTimeUTC>
        <ops:updateTimeUTC>1744470256299</ops:updateTimeUTC>
        <ops:cancelTimeUTC>0</ops:cancelTimeUTC>
        <ops:symptomCriticality>INFORMATION</ops:symptomCriticality>
        <ops:symptomDefinitionId>SymptomDefinition-1</ops:symptomDefinitionId>
        <ops:message>symptom message</ops:message>
        <ops:faultDevices/>
    </ops:symptom>
</ops:symptoms>

get.summary: Get Symptom Definition specified by id
get.description: Get Symptom Definition specified by id

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the Symptom Definition

Symptom definition with HT condition.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-2" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="HostSystem" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:name>Memory High Usage Percentage - only one state now allowed per symptom publicinition</ops:name>
    <ops:state severity="CRITICAL">
        <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:htCondition">
            <ops:key>mem|host_usagePct</ops:key>
            <ops:operator>GT_EQ</ops:operator>
            <ops:value>95</ops:value>
            <ops:valueType>NUMERIC</ops:valueType>
            <ops:instanced>false</ops:instanced>
            <ops:thresholdType>STATIC</ops:thresholdType>
        </ops:condition>
    </ops:state>
</ops:symptom-definition>

Symptom definition with LOG condition.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:symptom-definition id="SymptomDefinition-85cfae82-5d79-4bb5-90c7-5a7e394ebc7e" adapterKindKey="VMWARE" resourceKindKey="VirtualMachine" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:name>Log based symptom definition</ops:name>
    <ops:state severity="INFORMATION">
        <ops:condition xsi:type="ops:logCondition">
            <ops:logTriggerCondition>
                <ops:functionType>COUNT</ops:functionType>
                <ops:value>2.0</ops:value>
                <ops:operatorType>GREATER_THAN</ops:operatorType>
                <ops:timeInterval>5</ops:timeInterval>
                <ops:groupBy/>
            </ops:logTriggerCondition>
            <ops:queryTexts>
                <ops:queryText>filter_1</ops:queryText>
                <ops:queryText>filter_2</ops:queryText>
            </ops:queryTexts>
        </ops:condition>
    </ops:state>
</ops:symptom-definition>

delete.summary: Delete a Symptom Definition
delete.description: Delete a Symptom Definition

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Symptom Definition to delete

204.description: The symptom definition is deleted successfully

get.summary: Get a SuperMetric with the given id
get.description: Get a SuperMetric with the given ID

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Identifier of the SuperMetric

200.description: The Super Metric with the given identifier

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:supermetric xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:id>09a25e23-c63c-4fa1-be02-d23c5ef6e667</ops:id>

get.summary: Get the TopN stats for a given Resource and given a set of attributes
get.description: Get the TopN stats for a given Resource and given a set of attributes

parameters.description: The ID of the resource

parameters.name: topN
parameters.description:
The number of results. Keep in mind if a grouping contains multiple items (eg:
grouped by resource, with 4 metrics for the grouping resource) then returning the group with
4 metrics counts as 4 topN entries. If there are not enough remaining open topN entries to
return an entire group within the topN then the entire group is not returned. *

schema.description:
The number of results. Keep in mind if a grouping contains multiple items (eg:
grouped by resource, with 4 metrics for the grouping resource) then returning the group with
4 metrics counts as 4 topN entries. If there are not enough remaining open topN entries to
return an entire group within the topN then the entire group is not returned. *

parameters.description: The type of grouping, if not set defaults to ResourceStatGroupBy.RESOURCE

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: The type of sorting, if not set defaults to ResourceStatSort.ASCENDING

schema.description: The sort order

parameters.name: resourceId

parameters.name: statKey
parameters.description: Array of stat keys for which the stats should be returned

parameters.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

schema.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

        <ops:relatedResources/>
    </ops:resource>

<ops:notification-template templateId="9ab49928-e23c-47d7-992c-91f957048eea" templateType="ALERT" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

    <ops:description>WebhookPlugin Description</ops:description>
    <ops:contentConfigs>
        <ops:contentConfig>
            <ops:baseResourceKindKeyStats>
                <ops:resourceKindKey>
                    <ops:resourceKind>Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                    <ops:adapterKind>Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                </ops:resourceKindKey>
                <ops:stats>
                    <ops:stat>Property key</ops:stat>
                    <ops:stat>Stat key</ops:stat>
                </ops:stats>
            </ops:baseResourceKindKeyStats>
            <ops:ancestorResourceKindKeys>
                <ops:ancestorResourceKindKey>
                    <ops:resourceKindKey>
                        <ops:resourceKind>Ancestor Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                        <ops:adapterKind>Ancestor Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                    </ops:resourceKindKey>
                    <ops:stats>
                        <ops:stat>Ancestor Stat Key</ops:stat>
                        <ops:stat>Ancestor Property Key</ops:stat>
                    </ops:stats>
                </ops:ancestorResourceKindKey>
            </ops:ancestorResourceKindKeys>
            <ops:descendantResourceKindKeys>
                <ops:descendantResourceKindKey>
                    <ops:resourceKindKey>
                        <ops:resourceKind>Descendant Resource kind</ops:resourceKind>
                        <ops:adapterKind>Descendant Adapter kind</ops:adapterKind>
                    </ops:resourceKindKey>
                    <ops:stats>
                        <ops:stat>Descendant Property Key</ops:stat>
                        <ops:stat>Descendant Stat Key</ops:stat>
                    </ops:stats>
                </ops:descendantResourceKindKey>
            </ops:descendantResourceKindKeys>
        </ops:contentConfig>
    </ops:contentConfigs>
    <ops:formattingTemplate xsi:type="ops:webhookTemplate">
        <ops:newAlertTemplate>
            <ops:endpoint>/endpoint</ops:endpoint>
            <ops:headers>
                <ops:header>
                    <ops:key>Content-type</ops:key>
                    <ops:value>application/json</ops:value>
                </ops:header>
                <ops:header>
                    <ops:key>Header Key</ops:key>
                    <ops:value>Header Value</ops:value>
                </ops:header>
            </ops:headers>
            <ops:method>POST</ops:method>
            <ops:payload>{"startDate":"${CREATE_TIME}"}</ops:payload>
        </ops:newAlertTemplate>
    </ops:formattingTemplate>

get.summary: Get global settings metadata
get.description:
Find all the name-key mappings here:

Action History - ACTION_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
Deleted Objects - DELETED_OBJECTS_RETENTION_IN_HOURS,
Deletion Scheduling Interval - DELETION_SCHEDULING_INTERVAL_IN_HOURS,
Object History - OBJECT_HISTORY_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_MONTHS,
Session Timeout - SESSION_TIMEOUT_IN_MINUTES,
Symptoms/Alerts - CANCELLED_ALERTS_SYMPTOMS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Time Series Data Retention - TIME_SERIES_DATA_RETENTION_IN_MONTHS,
Additional Time Series Retention - ROLLUP_TIME_SERIES_DATA_RETENTION_IN_MONTHS,
Deleted Users - DELETED_USERS_RETENTION_IN_DAYS,
External Event Based Active Symptoms - OUTDATED_EVENT_BASED_SYMPTOMS_CANCELING_ENABLED,
External Event Based Active Symptoms - ACTIVE_EVENT_BASED_SYMPTOMS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Dynamic Threshold Calculation - DYNAMIC_THRESHOLDING_UPDATE_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Cost Calculation - COST_CALCULATION_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Deprecated from VMware Aria Operations 8.14: Customer Experience Improvement Program - TELEMETRY_ENABLED,
Allow vCenter users to log in to individual vCenters from VMware Cloud Foundation Operations - VC_USER_LOGIN_TO_INDIVIDUAL_VC_ALLOWED,
Allow vCenter users to log into VMware Cloud Foundation Operations from vCenter clients - VC_USER_LOGIN_FROM_VC_WEBUI_ALLOWED,
Allow vCenter users to log in to all vCenters from VMware Cloud Foundation Operations - VC_USER_LOGIN_TO_ALL_VC_ALLOWED,
System access URL - BASE_URL,
Automated Actions - AUTOMATED_ACTIONS_ENABLED,
Enable Standard Certification Validation - ENABLE_CERTIFICATE_VALIDATION_STANDARD_WAY,
Concurrent UI login sessions - ALLOW_CONCURRENT_LOGIN_SESSIONS,
Allow non-imported vIDM user access - ALLOW_NON_IMPORTED_VIDM_USERS_ACCESS,
Dynamic Threshold Calculation - DYNAMIC_THRESHOLDING_ENABLED,
Cost Calculation - COST_CALCULATION_ENABLED,
Deleted Objects - DELETED_OBJECTS_RETENTION_IN_HOURS_EXPANSION,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_ENABLED,
Docker image default registry URL - DOCKER_IMAGE_REGISTRY_URL,
Tag Based Pricing Metrics - PRICING_TAG_DETAILED_METRICS,
Tag based Costing Metrics - COSTING_TAG_DETAILED_METRICS,
Cluster Utilization Ceiling Factor - CLUSTER_UTILIZATION_ROUND_OFF,
Generated Reports Retention - GENERATED_REPORTS_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS,
Threshold For Adapters Certificate Expiration Alert - CERTIFICATE_ALARM_DAYS,
Show Cost Savings - SHOW_COST_SAVINGS_RECLAMATION_SETTING,
Include Powered off VMs for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_POWERED_OFF_VMS_ENABLED,
Include Powered off VMs for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_POWERED_OFF_VMS_DAYS,
Include Idle VMs for Reclamation - RECLAIM_IDLE_VMS_ENABLED,
Minimum number of days that VM has been idle for - RECLAIM_IDLE_VMS_DAYS,
Exclude VM provisioned in last - EXCLUDE_IDLE_VMS_PROVISIONED_IN_LAST_DAYS,
VM Idleness Criteria (MHz) - IDLENESS_INDICATOR_THRESHOLD_MHZ,
VM Idleness Criteria (%) - IDLENESS_INDICATOR_THRESHOLD_PERCENT,
Include Snapshots for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_SNAPSHOTS_ENABLED,
Include Snapshots for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_SNAPSHOTS_DAYS,
Include Orphaned Disks for Reclamation (flag) - RECLAIM_ORPHANED_DISKS_ENABLED,
Include Orphaned Disks for Reclamation (days) - RECLAIM_ORPHANED_DISKS_DAYS,
Orphaned Disks Collection Time - ORPHANED_VMDK_COLLECTION_TIME_IN_MINUTES,
Near Real-Time Monitoring Data Retention - NEAR_REAL_TIME_OBJECT_HISTORY_RETENTION,
Credential Ownership Enforcement - ACTIVATE_CREDENTIAL_OWNERSHIP.

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>

        <ops:name>Principal Credential</ops:name>

        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field key="VCADMIN" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
            <ops:field key="VCADMINPASSWORD" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>
    <ops:credential-kind id="HTTP">
        <ops:name>Http Credential</ops:name>
        <ops:adapterKindKey>ExampleAdapter</ops:adapterKindKey>
        <ops:fields>
            <ops:field key="UserType" defaultValue="User" valueType="string" required="false" enumerated="true">

                    <ops:enumeratedValue>Anonymous</ops:enumeratedValue>
                </ops:enumeratedValues>
            </ops:field>
            <ops:field key="Password" valueType="STRING" required="false" enumerated="false"/>
        </ops:fields>
    </ops:credential-kind>
</ops:credential-kinds>

get.summary: Retrieve detailed information about a particular authentication source
get.description:
The auth source can be SSO/LDAP/AD.
For the LDAP/AD auth source, the request should contain the name of the user.
For the SSO auth source, request should contain the name of the user and the sso domain.
User-group's username can contain wildcards. Maximum of 25 user-groups are returned for SSO auth source.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Id of the auth source

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:auth-source created="1744473856990" lastModified="1744473856990" xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:id>00000000-0000-000a-0000-000000000014</ops:id>
    <ops:name>TEST</ops:name>
    <ops:sourceType id="ACTIVE_DIRECTORY">
        <ops:name>ACTIVE_DIRECTORY</ops:name>
        <ops:description>DESC</ops:description>
    </ops:sourceType>
    <ops:name-value name="display-name">Test_11</ops:name-value>

delete.summary: Delete an authentication source
delete.description:
During the deletion of the SSO SAML/VCF SSO auth source, the same 'username' and 'password' which has been used while the creating this sso auth source, should be sent to the payload object.
Note: Force flag is applicable only for VCF SSO auth source. If force is true, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations continues to attempt to delete the VCF SSO auth source even when errors occur.
If you set it to false, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations stops attempting to removethe auth source if VCF SSO server fails

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Id of the auth source

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: This flag is applicable only to VCF SSO authentication sources.

requestBody.description: List of properties required for deletion of the auth source

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>

    <ops:name-value name="user-name">username</ops:name-value>
    <ops:name-value name="password">password</ops:name-value>
</ops:name-values>

delete.summary: Delete an existing Notification Plugin Instance
delete.description:
The field checkDependencies is added to preserve the initial behavior. By default it is set to false.

Case 1: When checkDependencies is false, the API has the following behavior:
Dependent Notification Rules are cascade deleted.
Dependent Report Schedules are unreferenced.
Dependent Tenant Email Configurations are always checked. If any then deletion is failed.

Case 2: When checkDependencies is true, the API has the following behavior:
All dependencies are checked. If any then deletion is failed.

To successfully delete the notification plugin, all conflicting dependencies must be removed.

parameters.name: pluginId
parameters.description: The identifier of the plugin instance

parameters.name: checkDependencies
parameters.description: Flag to check plugin dependencies

204.description: The notification plugin is deleted successfully

get.summary: Query for Resources within a particular Adapter Kind and Resource Kind
get.description:
Optionally filter these resources based on resource name. The resource name (specified as a query parameter) will be used for doing a partial match. However, if the resource identifiers and their values are specified, name is ignored and the API enforces all the mandatory (both uniquely identifying and required) identifiers are specified. This allows for looking up a single resource using a ResourceKey and allows the translation between a ResourceKey and a Resource UUID.

Example how to use the identifiers parameter (currently it cannot be used in our Swagger documentation):
/api/adapterkinds/{adapterKindKey}/resourcekinds/{resourceKindKey}/resources?identifiers[key1]=value1&identifiers[key2]=value2

parameters.name: adapterKindKey
parameters.description: The Adapter Kind Identifier

parameters.name: resourceKindKey
parameters.description: The Resource Kind Identifier

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Resource Name (partial match)

parameters.name: identifiers
parameters.description: Key,value pairs representing the resource identifiers of a Resource

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number from which data needs to be displayed (0-based)

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Expected number of entries per page

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

delete.summary: Delete (remove) a Resource as RelationshipType of a specific Resource
delete.description:
NOTE: Removing a relationship is not synchronous. As a
result, the delete operation may not happen immediately. It is recommended
to query the relationships of the specific Resource back to ensure that the
operation was indeed successful.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The UUID of the resource

parameters.name: relationshipType
parameters.description: Type of the Relationship that needs to be removed

parameters.name: relatedId
parameters.description: The UUID of the resource to remove from relationship

204.description: The relationship is deleted successfully

404.description: Either of the Resource that are part of the path parameters are invalid/non-existent

delete.summary: Delete the group type
delete.description: Delete the group type

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key of the group type to delete

200.description: The group type is deleted successfully

delete.summary: Delete multiple Resources
delete.description:
NOTE: Deletion of Resources is not synchronous. As a result,
the delete operation may not happen immediately. Furthermore, there is no way
to track the status of the deletion operation. It is recommended to query back
the system with the resource identifiers and ensure that the system returns a
404 error.

application/xml.example: No Custom Example is Available

204.description: If there is no error in starting the deletion of Resource(s)

500.description: If there is any error in starting the deletion of Resource(s)

The list of fault faultEvents that we are interested in tracking.
The symptom definition this condition is part of will be triggered if any of the fault faultEvents occurred.

eventType.description:
Represents the type of an Event.
All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

message.description:
The message is the string that the event's message must
either match exactly if operator is CompareOperator.EQ or
be a substring if operator is CompareOperator.CONTAINS
NOTE: Also in this case the message must be 3-128 characters long and the
symptom from the event must be IN the message

operator.description: Comparison Operator used within the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations Suite API

metric-event-condition.description:
Model object for a Metric Event Condition.
Implicitly maps to EventType#HARD_THRESHOLD.
The operator only allows for EXISTS conditions so specification of an operator
is not necessary and CompareOperator.EXISTS is implicitly used.

cancelCycles.description: The number of consecutive cycles to wait when a symptom state is false before a symptom is cancelled

realtimeMonitoringEnabled.description: Indicates whether real-time monitoring is enabled for the symptom or not. (This field is only visible in SaaS)

symptom-definition.description: A Symptom Definition defines conditions and properties which define when an alarm is triggered

symptom-state.description: The SymptomState is a product of the severity and a condition.  A symptom definition may have one SymptomState per severity level.

Score (value) associated with the Badge.
This number represents the absolute value of the Badge.
Typically the value is between 0-100 but this is not the
case all the time.

geo-location.description: Represents a geographical location on planet earth.

href.description:
The url part of this tag. Note that the href can be absolute or relative to the current url.
If the href begins with '/', then its absolute.
If the href begins with the protocol element(http, https), its absolute
Otherwise its relative.

rel.description: Represents the link's relation to its parent tag/object

link.description:
Represents a HTTP 'link' element.
It is implicit that all the link tags support http 'GET' method.

creationTime.description: Time the Resource was created in the system. This is a 'read-only' property and is system generated.The server ignores any changes to this field when trying to update a resource.

credentialInstanceId.description: Credential instance identifier assigned to this Resource, applicable only for adapter instances.

resourceStatusStates.description: The resource status and resource state for this resource as reported by one or more adapter instances. The server ignores any changes to this field when trying to update a resource.

resourceHealth.description: Enumeration that identifies the health of a Resource

resourceHealthValue.description: Resource Health Score. This is a 'read-only' property and its value is computed by the system. The server ignores any changes to this field when trying to update a resource.

dtEnabled.description: DT calculation enabled or not. By default DT calculation for a resource is enabled (during its creation).

monitoringInterval.description: Minutes part of the monitoring Interval of the resource, applicable only for adapter instances.

monitoringIntervalSeconds.description: Seconds part of the monitoring Interval of the resource, applicable only for adapter instances.

badges.description: The various major and minor badges and their values for a Resource. If certain badges are not present in the list then either those badges are not applicable to this resource or the calculation of those badges has been disabled for this resource.

identifier.description: Identifier of the Resource (typically a UUID).

isPartOfUniqueness.description: True if this ResourceIdentifier is used to uniquely identify the Resource in the system. By default the resource identifier is considered to be part of uniqueness

id.description: The recommendation id. Must be null during creation (System generated identifier)

recommendation.description:
The Recommendation encapsulates information and possible action needed to resolve a problem in the system.
The Recommendation optionally includes a RecommendedAction.

In other words, a recommendation may have textual data and no associated action
or it can have both.
The Recommendation is uniquely identified by an identifier
which is generated by the system during its creation.

actionAdapterKindId.description:
The Adapter Kind that defined this Recommended Action
An example could be the Out-of-the-box Remediation Adapter Kind ID

targetAdapterKindId.description:
The Adapter Kind for which this Recommended Action is applicable.
An example could be the Out-of-the-box VC Adapter Kind ID viz. "VMWARE"

targetResourceKindId.description:
Resource Kind of the Adapter Kind targetAdapterKindId
on which this Recommended Action is applicable.
For certain recommended actions, this can be null.
The system supports a scenario where the Recommended Action is only
associated with targetAdapterKindId only.
An example could be the Out-of-the-box VC Resource Kind ID viz. "VirtualMachine"

targetMethod.description:
Name of the remediation action that needs to be performed on the resource
An example could be a few of the VM lifecycle operations viz. "shutdown", "reset" etc.

recommended-action.description: Identifies an action the system may take to solve a problem.

If those fields are not provided, the value of newAlertRequestConfig will be used while sending the notification.

items.description: Sub Types of the alert

resourceId.description: Id of the resource that should be matched against the generated alert

matchResourceIdOnly.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases. Please use relationship instead.
If set to true, the rule engine does not evaluate the resource's children. Default value is: false.
NOTE: If set to true, childrenResourceKindKeyFilters has to be empty.

childrenResourceKindFilters.description:
If matchResourceIdOnly is set to false, this set allows the system to further filter children
based on their Resource Kind keys. This filter is deprecated. Please use childrenResourceKindKeyFilters instead.

items.description:
If matchResourceIdOnly is set to false, this set allows the system to further filter children
based on their Resource Kind keys. This filter is deprecated. Please use childrenResourceKindKeyFilters instead.

childrenResourceKindKeyFilters.description: Filter based on children resource kind keys.

relationship.description:
Relationship Types between resources. All relationships are many-to-many.
Representation names are plural.

ResourceFilter.description:
Filter for resources. If matchResourceIdOnly is specified, alerts are selected if their resource ids
match with the 'resourceId' specified in the filter. Otherwise, the system looks at all the resource's children.
The possible set of children can be further filtered based on the childrenResourceKindKeyFilters.

ResourceKindFilter.description: Filter based on the resource kind

name-value.description: Represents a single name -> value tuple.

id.description: Identifier of this rule, automatically assigned by the system on create

pluginId.description: Identifier of the plugin for which this rule applies

enabled.description: Identifier that shows if the notification rule is enabled. Enabled by default

templateId.description:
Identifier of the template.
NOTE: For deprecated plugin types this field will be ignored. For all other plugin types, default templates will be used if this value is not specified.

alertControlStates.description: Set of Alert control states that the alert is expected to be in

items.description: The control state of an Alert

alertStatuses.description: Set of Alert states that the alert is expected to be in

items.description:
Indicates the current condition of an Alert.
Used to determine if the conditions for the Alert still continue to exist.

criticalities.description: Set of Alert criticalities that the alert is expected to be in

items.description: The Criticality level

resourceKindFilters.description: Filter for the rule to be matched based on the Resource Kind for which the alert was generated

resourceFilters.description: Filters for the rule to be matched based on the "Resource and its Children" for which the Alerts were generated. This collection takes precedence over resourceFilter field.

alertTypeFilters.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts which are of the specified Alert type & Alert Subtypes

sendHeartbeat.description: Filter for the rule to send Alerts while they are active. Defaulted to False if nothing is provided.

properties.description:
Properties associated with the given rule. This may include information such as the email addresses of
receivers/etc...

collectorUUId.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts triggered on resources belonging to given collector

collectorGroupId.description: Filter for the rule to match Alerts triggered on resources belonging to given collector group

actionStatuses.description: Set of Action statuses that the action is expected to be in

items.description: Indicates the final status of an action.

notification-rule.description:
Model that represents a notification rule.
VMware Cloud Foundation Operations has a rule engine that evaluates these rules against generated alerts.
Actions such as sending Email, HTTP callback is done based on matching rules.
NOTE: resourceFilter and resourceKindFilter fields are deprecated, instead please use resourceFilters and resourceKindFilters fields respectively.

id.description: Identifier generated by the server for this schedule.

key.description: Schedule key provided by user. Cannot be null during creation.

maintenance-schedule.description: Represents a maintenance schedule during which resources are set to 'maintained' state. The server inturn informs the adapters that monitor these resources to stop monitoring until specified otherwise.Each maintenance schedule has an id, key and schedule. Both id and key have to be unique within the system. While id is automatically assigned by the server, key must be specified by the user.

hour.description: The beginning hour of the schedule.

minuteOfTheHour.description: The beginning minutes of the schedule.

duration.description: Duration of the schedule in minutes.

recurrence.description:
Configures how the schedule is repeated within the provided interval, e.g. if scheduleType = DAILY then setting recurrence = 2 would trigger the job every 2 days.
Applicable only when scheduleType = DAILY or scheduleType = WEEKLY.

dayOfTheMonth.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases.
Day of the month on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with daysOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

daysOfTheMonth.description: The list of the days of the month on which the job should be triggered. Applicable values are {1,2,...,31,LAST}. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

items.description: The list of the days of the month on which the job should be triggered. Applicable values are {1,2,...,31,LAST}. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and weeksOfTheMonth.

weeksOfTheMonth.description: The list of the week indices of the month on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and daysOfTheMonth, should be specified together with daysOfTheWeek.

items.description: Week of the Month

daysOfTheWeek.description:
Days of the week on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = WEEKLY or scheduleType = MONTHLY.
When scheduleType = MONTHLY this parameter is mutually exclusive with dayOfTheMonth and daysOfTheMonth, should be specified together with daysOfTheWeek.

items.description: Days of the Week

month.description:
Deprecated, will be removed after couple of releases.
Month of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with months.

months.description: Months of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with month.

items.description: Months of the year on which the job should be triggered. Must be specified only when scheduleType = MONTHLY. Mutually exclusive with month.

startDate.description: Start date for the schedule. Should be in format MM/DD/YYYY and if not specified current date is used.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the schedule. Should be in format MM/DD/YYYY. Mutually exclusive with expireRuns and either of them should be always defined when scheduleType != ONCE.

timeZone.description:
Time Zone of the schedule.
If not provided, defaults to the time of the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations server.

expireRuns.description: The number of run after which the schedule should expire. Mutually exclusive with expirationDate and either of them should be always defined when scheduleType != ONCE.

schedule.description: Schedule represents the meta information required by the server to set resources in maintenance mode at the right interval/time. To construct specific schedules, please see some of the static helper utilities in this class.

enabled.description: Indicates whether the logs should go to external log server or not

entities.description: Entities to forward the logs from

items.description: Enumeration of the Log Entity

host.description: The host to forward the logs to

port.description:
The Forwarding port


The port takes default value based on the protocol and whether SSL is enabled or not:
Protocol CFAPI, SSL disabled, Default port: 9000
Protocol CFAPI, SSL enabled, Default port: 9543
Protocol SYSLOG, SSL disabled, Default port: 514
Protocol SYSLOG, SSL enabled, Default port: 6514

useSSL.description: Flag that indicates whether SSL protocol is used or not

sslCertPath.description: The path to certificate authority file

log-forwarding-configuration.description: Represents the Log Forwarding Configuration object

rootLoggerLevel.description: The level of the root log

maxLogSize.description: This number specifies how much of log data to retain (MB)

id.description: ID of existing cloud account integration, must be null at creation

name.description: The name of the cloud account integration

description.description: Optional description of the cloud account integration

collectorId.description: The Collector instance used by this cloud account integration

collectorGroupId.description: The Collector Group ID to be used for this cloud account integration

resourceIdentifiers.description: Cloud account integration specific resource identifiers

certificates.description: List of new trusted certificates, must be provided upon creation/update of new cloud account integration, for establishing connection.

credentialInstanceId.description: Credential Instance identifier, for this cloud account integration.

CloudAccountIntegration.description: A model that serves as the structure of the payload data for creating or updating cloud account integration.

VCFIntegration.description: A model that serves as the structure of the payload data for creating or updating VCF integration

physicalDatacenterId.description: Physical Data center uuid for current VCF integration.

serviceCredentialsUsed.description: Flag that indicates whether service credentials are used or not.

thumbprint.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate,this field is used to represent the certificate guid

certificateDetails.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate, this field is used to represent certificate details. It also contains Algorithm, Signature (base64-encoded) and SignedCertificate (base64-encoded) values.

certificate.description: If the source returns an untrusted certificate, this field is used to represent the full certificate. Currently used only for VIDB and provided in PEM format.

issuedTo.description: The issuedTo date of the certificate

issuedBy.description: The issuedBy parameter of the certificate

auth-source.description:
Besides local users, VMware Cloud Foundation Operations also allows users from external systems such as LDAP, Active Directory, VC, SSO to
login using their original credentials. Authentication of all such users are delegated to external systems. This
model represents a single auth source managed by VMware Cloud Foundation Operations.

An Alert Definition state identifies the severity and impact a problem has when the

Defines a Problem by associating the Criticality level (Criticality)
of Symptom Definitions on related objects in the AlertDefinitionState
and also associates to a particular impact (AlertDefinitionImpact)

additionalProperties.description:
A map of recommendation identifiers keyed by recommendation id. A lower
priority value indicates higher priority. The priorities are required to
start from 1.

recommendationPriorityMap.description:
A map of recommendation identifiers keyed by recommendation id. A lower
priority value indicates higher priority. The priorities are required to
start from 1.

alert-definition-state.description:
The problem state describes the severity of a problem when specific symptoms exist on related resources. It optionally may include a prioritized recommendation list.
The Symptom Set can be either a {@link SymptomSet} or {@link CompositeSymptomSet}.

type.description: Used to identify schema type. For each schema its equivalent value should be used. allowableValues = {SYMPTOM_SET_COMPOSITE, SYMPTOM_SET}

symptom-query.description:
Query for Symptoms using various criteria.
Composition of filtering criteria controlled via the compositeOperator property.
Pagination of the Symptoms that are returned can be controlled via the PageInfo object.
Sorting of the Symptoms is specified via the SymptomField object and the SortOrder
object controls the order in which the Symptoms are sorted.
If a sort field is not specified but pagination is requested the Symptoms will be sorted
by the alarmId property.

time-range.description: Filter based on matching UTC cancel time

fault-alarm-device.description: Additional information of the faulted devices.

pageSize.description: Number of entries allowed in a page

sortBy.description: A CSV list of field names. Usually found in an enumeration, e.g. ResourceField

sortOrder.description: A CSV list of SortOrder values. If not specified or if list is shorter than sortFields then SortOrder.ASCENDING is assumed.

page-info.description: Represents page information for a paged result

updateTimeUTC.description: Last updated time of the symptom

cancelTimeUTC.description: Time when the symptom was canceled

kpi.description: True, if the symptom is a KPI, false otherwise

symptomDefinitionId.description: The Symptom Definition describing the violated rule for this Symptom

message.description:
The message associated with a symptom.

alarmInfo.description:
Additional information of the symptom.
NOTE: General recommendation is not to rely on the current format of the message content
since it can be changed by the source of alarm information at any time.
So, backward compatibility of the content of the message format is not guaranteed.

faultDevices.description: Additional information of the faulted devices.

begin.description:
The beginning date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Value measures the number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: If not provided, 24 hour rearward of ending date is used

end.description:
The ending date as a long value of the range of stats to return
Long value is number of milliseconds since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC
NOTE: The ending date must not be less than the beginning date.
If not provided, current time as UTC milliseconds is used

dt.description:
Indicates that the requested stats are based on some dynamic threshold.
Defaults to false which indicates that stats are not based on dynamic threshold.

latestMaxSamples.description:
The maximum number of samples to return when querying latest.
When processing the get latest stats query if it is omitted a value of 1 is assumed.
A query for the latest samples is not compatible with specifying the RollUpType value.

metrics.description: Indicates whether specified statKey-s of query should be forcibly treated as metrics (does not include instanced stats)

currentOnly.description:
Indicates whether to report only "current" stat values, i.e. skip the stat-s that haven't published any value
during recent collection cycles.

wrapStatValues.description:
Indicates whether stat values should be wrapped for application/xml response content type.
This flag is false by default and not needed for application/json response.
If set to true "statValues" will be returned instead of "values" for all content types.

Object used to define search criteria for retrieving Stats Data as well as Dynamic Thresholds

these are not specified the API returns 24 hours of Stats and DT data by default.

All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

resourceId.description: The id of the resource for the event

startTimeUTC.description:
The start time for the event in UTC.
This time must be after >= 946684800000L, or January 1, 2000. Default is 946684800000L.
Value is ignored for Change events.

Indicates that an event has been cancelled. For an externally managed event a new event with
the same message value must be sent with a cancelTime in order to cancel the
event.

Whether the HardThreshold event is a key performance indicator
True indicates it is a KPI. False otherwise.

managedExternally.description:
If set to true, this event shall persist across collection cycles until another event is pushed
to change the status.
If set to false, this event will be cancelled during the next collection cycle if it is not pushed again

capacity-allocation-settings.description:
Allocation Model differs to Demand Model as it does not consider Utilization. It is simply based on configured amount (e.g. 4 vCPU, 16 GB virtual RAM, 100 GB virtual Disk) that is allocated to VMs. As a result, Demand Model is still considered when Allocation is enabled.
Note: These settings will also apply to What-If Analysis, Committed Projects, and WLP calculations.

contextAdapterKindKey.description: The adapter kind of the context object that the action will be populated with. In other words, the kind of adapter that will be used when populating an action prior to execution. This may be different from the target resource, or the kind of the adapter that the action is executed against

contextResourceKindKey.description: The resource kind of the context object that the action will be populated with. In other words, the kind of resource that will be used when populating an action prior to execution. This may be different than the kind of resource that the action is executed against, or the target resource.

contextIds.description: The ids for the available Action Contexts for this action. Action Contexts are used in multi-step actions, with each id denoting a step in the overall action. Most actions will only have a single step, and this field may be ignored for those.

items.description: The ids for the available Action Contexts for this action. Action Contexts are used in multi-step actions, with each id denoting a step in the overall action. Most actions will only have a single step, and this field may be ignored for those.

scheduleEnabled.description: This field specifies whether the action can be scheduled

canRecommend.description: This field specifies whether the action can be used in recommendations. This is validated when an action is associated to a recommendation.
